ops.title: great-docs API reference is driven by a curated reference block in great-docs.yml; no __init__ facade, and TYPE_ALIAS kinds stay out of it
ops.body:
As of 2026-07-02 (commit a8e2074), `cc_pushback/__init__.py` is just a docstring — the old re-export facade + `__all__` (which this memory previously said to preserve) is gone. It existed only to steer great-docs, and the `scan` re-export shadowed the `cc_pushback.scan` submodule, crashing dynamic introspection ("Cannot find an object named ... cc_pushback:scan.discover_sidecars") and dropping `scan()` from the docs entirely.

**Why:** great-docs' auto-curation documents `cc_pushback.__all__` when no `reference:` block exists; a curated `reference:` block in `great-docs.yml` (module-path contents like `scan.scan`, `store.FeedbackStore`) bypasses the facade entirely and resolves unambiguously in both the static and dynamic passes.

**How to apply:** New public API surface → add its module path to the `reference:` block in `great-docs.yml`. Never re-introduce package-level re-exports for docs' sake. `type` aliases (e.g. `detectors.Detector`, and the old `SourceKind`/`DedupKey`) are Kind.TYPE_ALIAS and great-docs cannot render them — leave them out of the reference block. Don't git-pin great-docs to "fix" any of this; keep `great-docs>=0.13,<0.14`. Gate with `uv run great-docs build`; sync deps as `uv sync --extra dev --group docs` (a bare `--group docs` sync drops the dev extra, so `pytest` disappears). Related: [[fix-tooling-breakage-dont-punt]], [[cc-pushback-ecosystem-map]], [[no-tooling-meta-comments]].
